title: podman stop: do not cleanup for auto-removal
body:
Do not perform a container clean up for containers configured for
auto-removal (e.g., via `podman run --rm`).  There is a small race
window with the other process performing the removal where a clean up
during podman-stop may fail since the container has already been removed
and cleaned up.  As the removing process will clean up the container,
we don't have to do it during podman-stop.

Fixes: #7384
Signed-off-by: Valentin Rothberg <rothberg@redhat.com>

@mheon PTAL ... I couldn't reproduce the issue on my machine (I assume it's too fast)
Should we continue to remove the container and then just check if the container was already removed?

> Should we continue to remove the container and then just check if the container was already removed?

(*Container).CleanUp() doesn't remove the container but cleans up namespaces and other resources. The other process is already taking care of that, so it would be redundant to continue doing it in case of auto-removal.
